100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 13, 1926, the British government faced mounting pressure as labor strikes intensified, with coal miners demanding better wages and working conditions. In Paris, preparations were underway for the upcoming Olympic Games, set to showcase international athletic talent. Meanwhile, in the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition continued to stir public opinion, with some advocating for repeal amid rising crime rates linked to bootlegging.
Updated: Saturday, June 13, 2026 at 08:15:07 AM CDT
